_patch_meta.description: VC session VCSBFLAGS patch for vc-139 (G1345 dikaiōma, Registry 139) — raises 1 Session B observation (SBF-vc139-001) about the breadth of group 1099-001's characteristic span; Session B should evaluate whether the corpus splits into sub-zones.

record.description: Group 1099-001's revised context_description under v3_5 covers a wide characteristic span across 10 verses: (Zone A) standard disclosed-in inner righteousness — Luk 1:6, Rom 2:26, Rom 8:4; (Zone B) standard known-and-defied — Rom 1:32; (Zone C) verdict received — Rom 5:16, Rom 5:18; (Zone D) obsolete external regulation contrasted with inner reality — Heb 9:1, Heb 9:10; (Zone E) revealed acts eliciting worship — Rev 15:4; (Zone F) eschatological expression of inner righteousness — Rev 19:8. This was retained as a single group at VC per the default reuse rule (§6.2 Step 3) because characteristic-level splitting is Session B's analytical task. Session B should evaluate whether these zones constitute a unified characteristic (the dikaiōma as the outer pole of the inner/outer righteousness relation) or whether material divergence warrants splitting the group via a later VCREVISE. Relevant anchors: Rom 5:16 (verdict face) and Rev 15:4 (worship-response face).

operations.description: SBF-vc139-001 — Session B group-split-evaluation pointer for 1099-001 across 6 characteristic zones.
